comment_text.sn1.2:1.1: From here, the Pali gives only the abbreviated setting or none at all. In such cases, we are meant to read the full introduction per SN 1.1.
comment_text.sn1.2:5.1: The Buddha’s answer seems to mix a few lines of verse with some prose. This line is verse, as it has an unusual structure, best understood as due to the exigencies of metre, and where it occurs elsewhere it is always as verse. Also, this line and the next are metrical (vatta). The Chinese parallels at SA 1268 and SA-2 179 format the whole of the Buddha’s answer as prose, the verse being spoken by the deity at the end. Perhaps the Buddha quoted a couple of lines of verse in a prose sentence.
comment_text.sn1.2:5.2: With this the Buddha differentiates himself from Yājnavalkya, for whom perception (limited awareness) ends, but consciousness (infinite awareness) is eternal (Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 2.4.12).
comment_text.sn1.2:5.3: The Chinese parallels here speak of the liberation of the mind rather than the cessation of feelings. The textual problems seem insoluble and it appears the text is corrupt.

root_text.sn1.2:1.2: Atha kho aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā devatā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.2:2.1: “Jānāsi no tvaṁ, mārisa, sattānaṁ nimokkhaṁ pamokkhaṁ vivekan”ti?
root_text.sn1.2:3.1: “Jānāmi khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sattānaṁ nimokkhaṁ pamokkhaṁ vivekan”ti.
root_text.sn1.2:4.1: “Yathākathaṁ pana tvaṁ, mārisa, jānāsi sattānaṁ nimokkhaṁ pamokkhaṁ vivekan”ti?

translation_text.sn1.2:1.2: Then, late at night, a glorious deity, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn1.2:2.1: “Good fellow, do you know the liberation, emancipation, and seclusion of sentient beings?”
translation_text.sn1.2:4.1: “But how is it that you know the liberation, emancipation, and seclusion of sentient beings?”
translation_text.sn1.2:5.1: “Due to the ending of relish for rebirth;
translation_text.sn1.2:5.2: due to the finishing of perception and consciousness;
translation_text.sn1.2:5.3: due to the cessation and stilling of feelings—
translation_text.sn1.2:5.4: that, respectable sir, is how I know the liberation,
translation_text.sn1.2:5.5: emancipation, and seclusion of sentient beings.”
